title: VerbуВЛ уБиуВВуБкуБП Verb
titleEn: Used to express 'without a particular intention', 'unconsciously', or 'before one knows it'.
explanation: Used to express 'without a particular intention', 'unconsciously', or 'before one knows it'. (Ngu?n: Hanabira; b?n d?ch Vi?t c?n bi?n t?p.)
explanationEn: The phrase VerbуВЛуБиуВВуБкуБПVerb indicates doing something unintentionally or unconsciouslyтАФ you just find yourself doing it without a clear aim or intention.

title: VerbуВЛ уБиуВВуБкуБЧуБл Verb
titleEn: Expresses doing something unintentionally or without conscious thought.
explanation: Expresses doing something unintentionally or without conscious thought. (Ngu?n: Hanabira; b?n d?ch Vi?t c?n bi?n t?p.)
explanationEn: VerbуВЛуБиуВВуБкуБЧуБлVerb is used to show that you do some action without particularly intending to. Often you тАШend up doingтАЩ something by habit or casually.

title: VerbуВЛ уБкуВК
explanation: Expresses 'as soon as' or 'the moment'. (Ngu?n: Hanabira; b?n d?ch Vi?t c?n bi?n t?p.)
explanationEn: VerbуВЛуБкуВК indicates that the following action happens immediately after the verb action. It can be translated as 'no sooner than...', 'as soon as...', or 'the moment...'.

title: VerbуВЛ уБлуБиуБйуБ╛уВЙуБЪя╜ЮуВВ
titleEn: Expresses 'not onlyтАж but also', 'not limited toтАж'.
explanation: Expresses 'not onlyтАж but also', 'not limited toтАж'. (Ngu?n: Hanabira; b?n d?ch Vi?t c?n bi?n t?p.)
explanationEn: уБлуБиуБйуБ╛уВЙуБЪ is used to indicate that something goes beyond just the stated verb or noun. It can be translated as 'not justтАж but alsoтАж' or 'not limited toтАж'.

title: VerbуВЛ уБлуБпуБВуБЯуВЙуБкуБД
titleEn: Used to say 'there is no need to...', 'itтАЩs not necessary to...'.
explanation: Used to say 'there is no need to...', 'itтАЩs not necessary to...'. (Ngu?n: Hanabira; b?n d?ch Vi?t c?n bi?n t?p.)
explanationEn: VerbуВЛ уБлуБпуБВуБЯуВЙуБкуБД expresses that something is not worth the reaction or there is no need to do the verb. It's somewhat formal and often used in writing.

title: VerbуВЛ уБлуВВ
titleEn: Indicates that doing the verb is impossible or difficult, even if one wants to.
structure: Verb-dictionary form + уБлуВВ + (reason it can't be done)
structureEn: Verb-dictionary form + уБлуВВ + (reason it can't be done)
explanation: Indicates that doing the verb is impossible or difficult, even if one wants to. (Ngu?n: Hanabira; b?n d?ch Vi?t c?n bi?n t?p.)
explanationEn: VerbуВЛ уБлуВВ is often used with an additional clause explaining why the action canтАЩt be done. It can be translated as 'even if (I) want to do X, I canтАЩt (because...)'.

title: VerbуВЛ уБлуВВ Verb уВМуБкуБД
titleEn: Expresses: 'Even if I try/want to do something, I cannot.'
explanation: Expresses: 'Even if I try/want to do something, I cannot.' (Ngu?n: Hanabira; b?n d?ch Vi?t c?n bi?n t?p.)
explanationEn: This pattern emphasizes that a person wants or attempts to do something (often in volitional form), but is incapable of doing so. It is often used with potential verbs in negative form: 'Even if I try to ~, I can't ~.'

title: VerbуВЛ уБ╣уБЛуВЙуБЦуВЛ Noun
titleEn: Used to express what should not be done; 'must not', 'should not'.
explanation: Used to express what should not be done; 'must not', 'should not'. (Ngu?n: Hanabira; b?n d?ch Vi?t c?n bi?n t?p.)
explanationEn: The Japanese grammar point VerbуВЛ уБ╣уБЛуВЙуБЦуВЛ Noun is used to indicate something that should not be done, or is not allowed to be done. It translates to 'must not' or 'should not' in English. It is often used in formal written Japanese or in formal speeches. It is constructed by using the verb in its dictionary form followed by 'уБ╣уБЛуВЙуБЦуВЛ' and a noun, usually related to behavior or events.

title: VerbуВЛ уБ╣уБЛуВЙуБЪ
titleEn: Expresses the idea of 'must not do' or 'should not do'.
explanation: Expresses the idea of 'must not do' or 'should not do'. (Ngu?n: Hanabira; b?n d?ch Vi?t c?n bi?n t?p.)
explanationEn: The я╜ЮуВЛуБ╣уБЛуВЙуБЪ grammar point is used to express a strong prohibition or firmly established rule. It can be translated as 'must not do' or 'should not do'. It is common in formal or instructive settings (e.g., signs, notices). The formation is straightforward with verbs in dictionary form followed by уБ╣уБЛуВЙуБЪ.

title: VerbуВЛ уБ╣уБП
titleEn: Expresses the meaning of 'in order to', 'for the purpose of'.
explanation: Expresses the meaning of 'in order to', 'for the purpose of'. (Ngu?n: Hanabira; b?n d?ch Vi?t c?n bi?n t?p.)
explanationEn: The 'VerbуВЛ уБ╣уБП' grammar point is used to indicate a strong intention or purpose of the speaker. It can be translated as 'in order to' or 'for the purpose of' in English. It is more common in written language or formal contexts.
